headline: Letterman's Netflix Reunion: Comedy Legends Rekindle Late-Night Magic
lede: David Letterman reunited with comedy icons Martin Short and Paul Shaffer for a raucous Netflix event that celebrated their storied television partnership.
body:
The comedy veterans demonstrated their enduring chemistry during a candid conversation that blended hilarity with poignant personal revelations. Short and Shaffer joined Letterman for an evening that transported audiences back to the heyday of late-night television, offering an unvarnished look at their professional journey.

The trio's rapport remained electric, with Short showcasing his trademark musical comedy by performing an unexpectedly risqué tune alongside Shaffer. Their dynamic highlighted the deep creative bond forged during years of collaborative comedy on CBS's 'Late Show'.

Letterman, who retired from television in 2015, has found a new platform for storytelling through Netflix specials that allow for more intimate, unfiltered conversations. The event underscored his continued relevance in entertainment, proving that comedic legends never truly fade from the spotlight.

The reunion also touched on deeper themes of professional resilience, personal loss, and the transformative power of humor in navigating life's challenges. Each comedian shared candid moments that revealed the humanity behind their public personas.
key_facts: Netflix special reunites Letterman, Short, and Shaffer
Comedians discuss career breakthroughs and personal struggles
Short performed a comedic musical number
